nodes.title: ℹ️ About — BrainDead GLSL Skin Tinting
widgets_values:
# 🧠 BrainDead — GLSL Skin Tinting

GPU-accelerated GLSL batch shader — generate **4 skin tone variations** (light / medium / dark / zombie) from a single source image in **one pass**.

## Shader
The **skin_shader.glsl v2.27** is embedded directly in the  field of . No external file needed — the workflow is self-contained.

4 outputs per run:
- **fc0** → ILM (Unreal + GG Manga Toon: R=spec, G=shadow, B=rim, A=body)
- **fc1** → SR Preview (exact Unity composite of fc2 + flat skin tint)
- **fc2** → Unity MainTex (RGB=art, A=tint mask — replaces MainTex in skin shader graph)
- **fc3** → Unreal UEFN albedo (desaturated skin zone, original clothing)

## Flow
1. **Load Image** — character render (RGBA recommended; alpha = body mask).
2. **BD GLSL Batch** — skin_shader.glsl runs 4× via  (u_int3/4/5 = skin tint RGB per tone). Single GPU compile, 4 framebuffer outputs per iteration.
3. **BD Save Batch** —  +  → one PNG per tone.

## Customising skin tones
Edit  in **BD GLSL Batch**:

These are **MIDTONE** values (V-curve alpha=0 zone). Shadow/highlight are derived by the shader from the tint.

## Recommended save wiring per fc channel
| fc | save_only | Why |
|----|-----------|-----|
| fc1 | (empty) | All 4 tones differ — save all |
| fc0 |  | Shadow path differs only for zombie |
| fc2 |  | Same |
| fc3 |  | Tone-independent — one albedo per character |

## VRAM
Runs entirely on GPU via GLSL ES 3.0 — no AI model loaded.

---
**BrainDeadGuild** — created by **BizaNator**  
[BrainDeadGuild.com](https://BrainDeadGuild.com) · [BrainDead.TV](https://BrainDead.TV) · [GitHub](https://github.com/BizaNator/ComfyUI-BrainDead) · [Discord](https://braindeadguild.com/discord)

u_float0=0.5      // V-curve midpoint (where alpha=0, pure SkinColor tint shows)
u_float1=0.3      // V-curve power (0.5=sqrt sharp, 1.0=linear ramp)
u_float2=0.40     // Outer band — luma distance from mid where alpha=1.0
u_float3=0.8      // Highlight overlay strength (reads u_image3.G channel)
u_float4=0.6      // Body mask threshold
u_float5=1.0      // Dark overlay strength (reads u_image3.B channel)
u_float6=0.5      // Line opacity (0=invisible, 1=fully opaque)
u_float8=0.5      // Skin desaturation amount (1.0=full grey, 0.0=keep colour)
u_float15=0.85    // RGB shadow depth
u_float16=1.00    // Shadow map strength (u_image3.R)
u_float17=1.00    // RGB highlight push

u_int1=0      // Line color: 0=black, 1=white
u_int2=1      // Line mask: 0=off, 1=on (reads u_image3.alpha, white=line)
u_int7=0      // Source: 0=original.rgb, 1=mannequin u_image4
u_int12=1     // u_image2 mode: 0=ignore (compute grey from src), 1=use pre-baked grey

// MIDTONE values from SKIN_TONES_RULES.MD (light / medium / dark / zombie)
u_int3=166,139,66,74        // Skin Tint R
u_int4=124,69,40,106        // Skin Tint G
u_int5=99,61,34,58          // Skin Tint B
